concise_definition: 3 out 2 in motion offense : Basketball offensive strategy that generally focuses on creating various scoring opportunities via the initial formation of three offensive players located outside near perimeter areas, typically beyond the three-point line, and two offensive players located inside near the low post areas.

sections.heading: What is the 3 out 2 in motion offense in basketball?
sections.text:
The 3 out 2 in motion offense is a basketball strategy that utilizes a variety of offensive actions based on an initial formation of three perimeter players, typically located near the top and wing areas, as well as two interior players near the low post areas.

In essence, the 3 out 2 in motion offense seeks to create a general balance of inside and outside high percentage scoring opportunities. By focusing on various offensive actions such as ball screens, dribble handoffs, and the high low tactic, this scheme can be beneficial to both guards and post players while simultaneously presenting a notable defensive challenge for the opposition.

sections.heading: How to understand the player roles and diagrams on this page
sections.text:
In reference to any diagrams below, each of the offensive players are generally represented by the standardbasketball positionsin number format, unless otherwise noted.

In essence, 1 is thepoint guard; 2 is theshooting guard; 3 is thesmall forward; 4 is thepower forward; 5 is thecenter.

It should also be mentioned that it’s possible for players to switch locations during the initial alignment. For example, the shooting guard and power forward could switch places, which would have the shooting guard in one of the low post areas while the power forward occupies one of wing areas.

Nevertheless, the overall premise remains the same: three perimeter players and two interior players.

Additionally, in terms of a brief legend for the diagrams:

- Black Dot or Small Circle:The black dot or small circle represents the basketball.

- Solid Arrow:The solid arrow indicates movement or a cut on the basketball court.

- Dotted Arrow:The dotted arrow represents a basketball pass.

- Wavy Arrow:The wavy arrow indicates dribbling.

- T-Shaped Symbol:The T-shaped symbol signifies a screen, also known as a pick.

- Double pipe symbol:The double pipe symbol denotes handoff action.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring ball screen action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring ball screen action that also includes a unique lane cut, which could lead to additional scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble entry
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing dribble entry as well as players moving towards corner areas.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing dribble entry as well as players moving towards corner areas.]

To begin, 1 performs a dribble entry towards the left side wing. As that happens, 3 shifts down to the left side corner while 4 cuts out to the right side corner.

sections.heading: Part 2: Ball screen and lane cut
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates a ball screen as well as lane cut action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates a ball screen as well as lane cut action.]

Next, 5 lifts up to set a ball screen and 1 uses it to dribble towards the middle of the court. After that, 2 receives the ball from 1.

Immediately after 2 has possession of the ball, 3 moves slightly up around the adjacent wing before quickly cutting in towards the left side lane line.

If the timing is good, 3 should receive the ball from 2 in the moment that one or both feet touch the lane area just below the free throw line.

From there, 3 can take a mid-range jump shot or shoot a floater in the lane if feasible.

Additionally, 3 could attack the rim in an attempt to score or even throw a kick out pass with 4 as the target if that is open.

It should also be mentioned that this particular lane cut from the corner/wing area is sometimes referred to as a Corey cut or Maggette cut.

It’s derived from the name of former NBA player,Corey Maggette, who would use that type of cut to get open and score around the lane area or directly at the basket.

Related:The Corey Cut: An Off the Ball Basketball Cutting Concept – YouTube

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring a dribble handoff?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring a dribble handoff in addition toscreen the screener action, which could result in a scoring opportunity near the basket or around the perimeter.

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble handoff and down screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing a dribble handoff and down screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing a dribble handoff and down screen.]

To start, 1 performs dribble handoff action with 3 near the right side wing area.

As that happens, 4 cuts to the left side wing via adown screenset by 2. Additionally, after setting the screen, 2 fills the vacant left sidelow postarea.

From there, 3 continues to dribble back to the top area followed by a pass towards 4, indicated by the dotted arrow.

sections.heading: Part 2: Cross screen and down screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense demonstrating a cross screen and down screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense demonstrating a cross screen and down screen.]

Next, 5 cuts across the lane to the left side low post area via across screenset by 2.

At the same time, 2 cuts to the top via a down screen set by 3. Continuing on, 4 has the option to pass the ball with 5 or 2 as intended receivers.

From there, 5 could score via a low post move or 2 could take the open three-point shot, respectively.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring dribble weave action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring dribble weave action alongsideroll and replace, which could create a potentialperimeterorclose-rangescoring opportunity.

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble Weave
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing dribble weave action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense showcasing dribble weave action.]

To begin, 1 performs a dribble handoff with 3 near the left sidewingarea.

Afterwards, 3 continues to dribble towards the top and eventually performs a dribble handoff with 2 near the right side wing.

From there, 2 dribbles back towards the top, completing the dribble weave action.

Also, as that happens, 1 and 3 cut down towards the left side and right sidecorners, respectively.

sections.heading: Part 2: Roll and replace
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense demonstrating roll and replace action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense demonstrating roll and replace action.]

Next, 2 dribbles toward the basket via the high pick set by 5 who also rolls to the rim. Additionally, as that occurs, 4 replaces at the top.

Following that, 2 could take the mid-range shot or pass to either 4 or 5, which could lead to a potential three-point shot or layup, respectively.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring flex action?
sections.text:
This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that features flex action alongside adribble entryand a flare screen.

Related:3 Out 2 In Flex Offense

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble entry
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows dribble entry action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows dribble entry action.]

To start, 1 dribbles toward the right side wing. As that occurs, 2 cuts through thebaselineto the left side corner.

Moreover, 3 replaces at the top, 5 replaces at the left side wing, and 4 replaces at the left side low post area.

sections.heading: Part 2: Flex and flare
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that includes a flex screen and flare screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that includes a flex screen and flare screen.]

Next, 2 cuts back toward the basket via aflex screenset by 4. While that happens, 3 cuts away from the ball towards the left side wing via theflare screenset by 5.

2 could receive the ball from 1, which could result in a layup around the basket.

Alternatively though, 3 could receive it instead via askip passfrom 1 and then take the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring high low action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that features high low action in addition to handoffs, resulting in potential scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble handoff and high low
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases dribble handoff and high low action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases dribble handoff and high low action.]

To start, 1 dribbles toward the right side wing area to execute a dribble handoff alongside 2 who dribbles back toward the top area.

Afterwards, 3 receives possession of the ball from 2 and while that happens, 5 flashes up to the high post area to receive the ball from 3.

From there, 4 could quickly duck into the lane area while potentially sealing the defender behind, receive the ball from 5 viahigh lowaction, and score around the basket if possible.

Otherwise, 5 could keep possession of the ball while 4 cuts through to the right side corner.

sections.heading: Part 2: Handoff
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases a possible handoff near the high post area.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases a possible handoff near the high post area.]

Next, 5 could pivot slightly away from the direction of the basket towards the left side elbow area and while that happens, 2 could cut towards 5 to receive a possible handoff.

From there, 2 could attack the rim and score around the basket. Alternatively, if 2 does not receive the ball from 5, then 2 could simply cut through to the left side corner.

sections.heading: Part 3: Dribble handoff and roll
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases dribble handoff and roll action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases dribble handoff and roll action.]

Continuing on, 5 dribbles toward the left side wing to perform a dribble handoff alongside 3.

After that, 3 dribbles toward the middle of the court near the high post area while 5 rolls to the basket.

Moreover, 2 could lift up from the left side corner to the adjacent wing, similar to typicalshake action.

Following that, 3 could take the mid-range shot or pass the ball to any of the other teammates, depending on whoever could be open for scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring multiple screening actions?
sections.text:
This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring multiple screening actions that could lead to scoring opportunities near the basket or around the perimeter.

It should also be noted that the initial setup is slightly different than from the typical alignment. In essence, 1 starts at the top as usual but 3 and 4 occupy the wings. Moreover, 2 and 5 fill the low post areas.

sections.heading: Part 1: Cross screen and exchange cuts
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates a cross screen and exchange cuts.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates a cross screen and exchange cuts.]

To begin, 4 receives the ball from 1 and as that happens, 2 cuts into the lane to set a cross screen with 5 as the recipient.

From there, 5 could potentially receive the ball from 4 and possibly score via a low post move. Furthermore, 2 should cut out towards the right side short corner, which also gives 5 more space to operate down low.

Additionally, while that occurs, 1 and 3 execute exchange cuts on the weak side of the floor, primarily to occupy the defense.

On the other hand, if 5 is not open, then 3 could receive the ball instead from 4.

sections.heading: Part 2: Post screens
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates post screens for a potential three-point shooter.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates post screens for a potential three-point shooter.]

Next, 2 cuts toward the left side wing via screens near the low post and mid-post areas set by 5 and 4, respectively.

Afterwards, 2 receives the ball from 3 and takes the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: Part 3: Alternative screen the screener
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates screen the screener action, which could lead to a three-point shot.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates screen the screener action, which could lead to a three-point shot.]

Here is an alternative option, which includes screen the screener action, leading to a potential three-point shot, particularly for the mid-post screener.

Essentially, 2 cuts toward theperimetervia the post screens, similar to the previous diagram.

However, instead of cutting up to the left side wing to receive the ball, 2 turns within the three-point arc to set a down screen with 4 as the recipient.

Following that, 4 uses that screen, cuts to the left side wing area, receives the ball from 3, and takes the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring multiple scoring options?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates multiple scoring options, particularly emphasized for the team’s wing players.

sections.heading: Part 1: Dribble entry and through cut
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a dribble entry and through cut.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a dribble entry and through cut.]

To begin, 1 performs a dribble entry towards the left side wing. As that happens, 2 cuts through to the empty right side corner.

Additionally, 4 lifts up from the left side low post area to fill the recently vacated top area.

sections.heading: Part 2: Wing cut
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a shuffle-type cut from the wing.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a shuffle-type cut from the wing.]

Next, 3 performs a shuffle-like cut from the wing towards the lane area via the off-ball screen that is set by 5 just outside of the right elbow area.

3 could receive the ball from 1 if open and score around the rim. Otherwise, 1 could hold possession of the ball while 3 fills the adjacent left side low post area.

sections.heading: Part 3: Stagger
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a stagger screen, which could result in a potential three-point shot.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows a stagger screen, which could result in a potential three-point shot.]

Following the previous action, 2 could cut towards the top via the stagger screen set by 5 and 4, respectively.

After that, 2 could receive the ball from 1 and take the three-point shot if that is open.

sections.heading: Part 4: Elevator
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows an elevator screen, which could result in a potential three-point shot.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows an elevator screen, which could result in a potential three-point shot.]

If 2 received the ball but is not open to take the shot, then 3 could cut back towards the original right side wing area by way of an elevator screen set by 5 and 4, respectively.

From that point, 3 could receive possession of the ball from 2 and take the three-point shot. Also, 4 and 5 could try to gather an offensive rebound in the event that 3 misses the three-pointfield goalattempt.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring pass and screen away action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuringpass and screen awayaction alongside additional screens.

sections.heading: Part 1: Pass and screen away
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates pass and screen away action alongside a cross screen in the lane.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that demonstrates pass and screen away action alongside a cross screen in the lane.]

To begin, 2 receives the ball from 1 and afterwards, 3 cuts to the top via screen away action by 1.

Furthermore, as that happens, 5 cuts across the lane to the left side low post area by way of a cross screen set by 4.

Following the screening actions, 4 clears out to the right side corner while 1 fills the right side wing.

From that point, 5 could receive the ball and score via a layup or low post move. Alternatively, 3 could receive it and take the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: Part 2: Ram screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that features a ram screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that features a ram screen.]

If 3 gains possession of the ball but the shot is not there, then 1 could receive the ball near the right side wing.

Next, 5 could cut towards the ball via theram screenset by 3, who also fills the left side corner.

After that, 1 could dribble towards the lane via theon-ball screenset by 5, who also rolls to the basket.

What’s more, as that happens, 4 fills the vacated right side wing area viashake action.

From there, 1 could attempt to score or pass to other teammates, which could result in more possible scoring options.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring pick and roll action?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that features pick and roll action to generate one or more scoring opportunities.

sections.heading: Part 1: Back screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that initially shows a back screen.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that initially shows a back screen.]

To begin, 1 dribbles toward the right slot and as that happens, 4 and 5 lift to the high post elbow areas while 2 shifts down to the left side corner area.

Following that, 3 receives the ball from 1 and afterwards, 1 cuts to the basket via the back screen set by 5.

From there, 1 could gain possession of the ball again and score around the basket. Otherwise, 1 could cut through to the right side corner.

sections.heading: Part 2: Pick and roll
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows pick and roll action with a wide pin down screen on the weak side.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that shows pick and roll action with a wide pin down screen on the weak side.]

Next, 3 dribbles toward the middle of the court via a side pick set by 5, who also rolls to the basket.

At the same time, 2 cuts toward the wing by way of the wide pin down screen set by 4, who also pops to the left side short corner area.

In addition, 1 could shake towards the previously occupied right side wing area.

From that point, 3 could take the mid-range jump shot or pass to one of the other teammates depending on whoever is open.

sections.heading: What is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring a quick hitter?
sections.text: This is an example of 3 out 2 in motion offense featuring a quick hitter withoff-ball screensto create a possible scoring option around the basket as well as near the perimeter area of the court.

sections.heading: Part 1: Back screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases a back screen within quick hitter action.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases a back screen within quick hitter action.]

To start, 1 passes the ball with 2 as the targeted receiver. After that, 1 cuts to the basket by way of a back screen set by 4.

From there, 1 could receive the ball again from 2 and score via a layup if that is open.

Conversely, 1 could cut out of the lane towards the left side low post area while 4 pops to the previously occupied top area.

sections.heading: Part 2: Triple screen
sections.text:
[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases triple screens.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of 3 out 2 in motion offense that showcases triple screens.]

Next, 2 couldreverse the ballwith 4 as the intended recipient. Immediately afterwards, 2 cuts across the court from the left side wing towards the right side wing by way of the triple screen set by 1, 5, and 3, respectively.

2 could receive the basketball once again via another reversal from 4 and take the open three-point shot.

sections.heading: What is an example of an entry for the 3 out 2 in motion offense?
sections.text:
This is an example of a very simple entry that can quickly flow into the 3 out 2 in motion offense.

[Image Alt Text: An example of a box entry that can quickly flow into the 3 out 2 in motion offense.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of a box entry that can quickly flow into the 3 out 2 in motion offense.]

In essence, the initial set starts with theboxformation. 1 is at the top; 4 and 5 are on the high post elbows; 2 and 3 fill the low post areas.

To start the entry action, 4 and 5 cut towards the low post areas to set down screens. 2 and 3 use those screens respectively to get open towards the wing areas.

From there, 1 has the option to pass to either 2 or 3 while 4 and 5 fill the vacant low post areas following the screening action.

After 2 or 3 receives the ball, either player can take the open three-point shot if that is feasible or execute some other action within the 3 out 2 in motion offense.

sections.heading: What are general advantages of the 3 out 2 in motion offense?
sections.text:
The primary advantage of the 3 out 2 in motion offense is that it could be very beneficial for teams with two or more low post players that are capable of scoring near the basket.

Additionally, since there are typically two post players near the basket, this offers offensive teams a greater likelihood to rebound their own misses for second chance points.

sections.heading: What are general disadvantages of the 3 out 2 in motion offense?
sections.text:
One notable disadvantage of the 3 out 2 in motion offense is that it could be more challenging toattack the rim, particularly by way of perimeter dribble drive action when the two low post players are on each post block.

Essentially, if a perimeter player attacks the rim, especially with the intention of scoring by way of alayupor dunk, then the defenders that are guarding the low post players could potentially act ashelp defendersto decrease scoring possibilities for that same perimeter player.

Also, the 3 out 2 in motion offense might not be as useful forundersized basketball teams.

Basically, if a team has low post players that are undersized, then it may be more challenging for those same post players to score, particularly with low post moves when the low post defenders are bigger, taller, and/or stronger.
